title: Why Micron Stock Keeps Going Up
text: Micron Technology has become a momentum stock driven by both strong financial performance and positive headlines. The company reported a 196% surge in sales and a 771% increase in earnings last quarter, supporting its rally rather than relying solely on hype. CNBC profiled Micron's remarkable ascent, noting that shares are up nearly 150% so far this year. The outlet describes customers as scrambling to purchase computer memory chips, competing intensely for available inventory. The company's expected gross profit margin exceeds 75%, surpassing even Nvidia's 71% margin and creating what analysts term a supercycle in memory chips. However, the article warns of cyclical industry dynamics. While the semiconductor sector currently favors Micron, the author cautions that competitors will eventually challenge its dominance, just as they have with Nvidia's AI chip leadership. This competitive pressure could trigger a significant downturn once the current cycle peaks. The underlying message: investors should maintain a long-term perspective and recognize that today's exceptional profitability may not persist indefinitely in this historically volatile industry.
